mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over a 6-month period indicates rising inflation expectations, leading to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over a 6-month period indicates rising economic activity, leading to higher demand for raw materials and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Housing Starts Index (HOUST) over a 3-month period signals rising housing demand, leading to higher economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rising housing demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over a 3-month period signals an easing monetary cycle, leading to higher economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Nonfarm Payrolls Index (PAYEMS) over a 3-month period signals rising labor demand, leading to higher economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rising labor demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Real Disposable Personal Income Index (RSAFS) over a 6-month period indicates rising consumer spending, leading to higher economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-spending periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
